title: Vice President, Corporate Functions Human Resources Business Advisor
description:
Step into an exciting opportunity in Singapore as a Corporate Functions Human Resources (HR) Business Advisor. This role allows you to shape impactful HR strategies and build strong relationships with key business stakeholders. You'll be part of a dynamic team that values innovation and professional growth, offering you the chance to advance your career in a global environment. Experience the benefits of joining a leading firm that supports your development and success. Be ready to make a significant impact and grow with us.

As a Vice President, HR Business Advisor within our Human Resources team, you will work closely with clients in Corporate Functions. You will provide strategic HR advisory and support, thriving in a fast-paced, demanding environment. You will execute strategic priorities set by senior leaders and collaborate with managers at various levels to drive organizational success. You will engage with senior HR Business Advisors and managers to resolve HR-related matters. Your role involves implementing HR policies, programs, and services effectively.

In this role, you will own relationships with key business stakeholders, offering expert advice across a full spectrum of HR activities. You will design collaborative solutions for emerging people matters, ensuring alignment with regulatory and corporate policies. You will operate effectively within a highly matrixed organization, leveraging resources from multiple areas to deliver results. Your contributions will make a meaningful impact on our firm and the wider community. Join us and be part of a dynamic and supportive environment that values your expertise.

Job Responsibilities:

• Consult with business stakeholders on HR activities, including organizational design, employee relations, compensation, staffing, and talent management.

• Provide creative and pragmatic advice to manage human capital issues, ensuring compliance with regulatory and corporate policies.

• Coordinate talent management programs, identifying future leaders, succession plans, and diverse talent.

• Offer Employee Relations advice, supporting managers on flexible working, grievances, disciplinary issues, and redundancies.

• Coordinate the compensation process, ensuring data integrity and educating managers on compensation philosophy.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills:

• Minimum 8 years of overall HR experience

• Minimum Bachelor’s degree or equivalent.

• Proven experience in HR advisory roles within a financial services environment.

• Ability to navigate a global matrix organization and partner on issues across HR and the business. Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build relationships with stakeholders.

• Knowledge of HR policies, procedures, and best practices.

• Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a dynamic environment.

• Exceptional judgment, demonstrated ability to make sound decisions and be creative in developing alternative solutions in a fast-paced environment. Ability to utilize critical thinking and analytical skills regularly to identify issues and trends, develop solutions and/or recommendations to address root cause

• Strong analytical, consulting, quantitative and technical skills that enable individual to leverage data to create HR strategies that support and drive business results. Project management abilities including execution skills and end to end process improvement. Technically proficient in MS Office Suite – including Excel and PowerPoint

• Ability to communicate professionally and effectively, at all levels of the business;

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills:

• Strong influencing capability to promote and sell ideas to drive change;

• Resilience in a constantly changing environment;

• Strong generalist knowledge of human resources processes, principles, concepts and methodology in a business setting, across goal setting, performance assessment and improvement, compensation practices, talent review and talent management, workforce planning, reductions in force, manager development

• Able to manage multiple projects, understand the business and execute efficiently.

To apply for this position, please use the following URL:

https://ars2.equest.com/?response_id=56cd1b2eea4c9360ef4f5d3edd598b7c

postedCompany.description:
About J.P. Morgan

J.P. Morgan has been a leader in helping companies grow and markets develop throughout our history. We collaborate across the globe to deliver the solutions and advice to meet our clients’ needs, anywhere in the world.  We operate in 100 countries and hold global leadership positions across our businesses. When you join J.P. Morgan you’ll be: working with a team committed to being the best; earning the trust of our clients; demanding excellence of yourself.

What makes this the best place to build your career? Because we’ll make the most of all your talents, not just your technical skills. We’ll challenge you, but we’ll also make being here as stimulating and diverse as your life is. You’ll be given the tools, training and support needed to help us keep doing first-class business in a first-class way, as we have for over 200 years. Our global platform allows you to have more mobility, choices and opportunities to contribute than you ever imagined. It all adds up to the perfect opportunity to take your career further with us.
postedCompany.name: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.
